title: Etsy GMS continues decline in Q4 as ads boost revenue
text: Etsy reported a fifth consecutive quarterly decline in gross merchandise sales (GMS) during Q4 2024. Consolidated GMS fell 6.8% year-over-year to $3.74 billion, down from $4.01 billion in the prior year period. The core Etsy marketplace experienced sharper declines, with GMS dropping 8.6% to $3.3 billion. The company attributed weakness to weak discretionary spending, a shorter holiday season, shifts in category mix, and intense promotional competition. Despite declining GMS, Etsy achieved record revenue of $852.2 million in Q4, a 1.2% increase year-over-year. Full-year 2024 revenue reached $2.81 billion, up 2.2% from 2023. This divergence was driven primarily by advertising revenue from Etsy Ads. CEO Josh Silverman stated the company is moving with urgency to increase buyer engagement, drive more sales, and return to GMS growth. Active buyers on the marketplace declined 2.6% to 89.6 million. Active sellers experienced a sharper drop of 20% to 5.6 million, attributed to new onboarding fees and enhanced trust and safety initiatives. Depop, Etsy's fashion resale subsidiary, achieved its strongest GMS growth since acquisition, with 2024 full-year GMS climbing nearly 32% to $788.9 million.
